picks.title: Homelanding
picks.blurb: A free summer festival on the waterfront Marineterrein — food, music, a swim spot and design across the old naval yard.

picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: GRAMMY-winning pop sensation brings his first full stadium tour to Amsterdam, with DJ Pee Wee and Victoria Monét.

picks.title: VBX Summer Weekend
picks.blurb: Rey Colino, Paquita Gordon, Muallem, Ion Ludwig and more at TILLATEC.

picks.title: PRNCPTL DOES AMSTERDAM - DAY & NIGHT
picks.blurb: CRYME, DJ Fuckoff, SALOME, Slimfit and more at Lofi.

picks.title: TREK Amsterdam (Street Food Festival)
picks.blurb: Open-air restaurant with global streetfood, beer garden, live music, theatre and late-night disco.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: Love on the Canals – 30 Years of Canal Parade
picks.blurb: Pride history through archival photos, art, and personal stories celebrating 30 years of Amsterdam's iconic boat parade.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: Hortus Summer Tour
picks.blurb: Expert-guided walk through Amsterdam's most beautiful garden spotlighting rare plants, greenhouses, and hidden corners with live music.
picks.why: Summer tour on weekend; unique botanical experience

picks.title: Hortus Zomeravonden
picks.blurb: Botanical garden stays open Sunday evenings in July; join guided summer tours discovering rare plants, greenhouses and hidden gems.

picks.title: The Sheep Detectives
picks.venue: Filmhallen, Pathé Bioscopen, Ketelhuis, Vué, Studio/K, The M
picks.blurb: Clever sheep secretly solve farm mysteries inspired by detective stories, embarking on adventures filled with clues and suspicious characters.

picks.title: Garlic Festival at VRR
picks.blurb: Garlic-themed market and three-course dinner celebrating the ingredient with live music and stalls.

picks.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto
picks.blurb: Twenty years later, Miranda Priestly and Andy navigate a transformed fashion world that's faster, more digital, and ruthlessly competitive.
picks.why: Fashion drama reunion decades in making

picks.title: American Myth Memory – David Levinthal Photographs
picks.blurb: Photographer David Levinthal examines American cultural mythology through evocative photographs spanning decades of work.

picks.title: Liefde op de Grachten – 30 Jaar Canal Parade
picks.blurb: Celebrate Canal Parade's 30-year anniversary with photos, art and personal stories showing Pride's evolution into a global celebration.

picks.title: Man in the Mirror
picks.blurb: Michael Jackson tribute with live band, dancers, iconic moves and costume recreating the King of Pop's greatest hits.

picks.title: Spinoza
picks.blurb:
Amsterdam, 17th century. 
 A city that sees itself as a safe haven. 
 A Portuguese-Jewish refugee community. 
 Three children. One family. 
 About Spinoza
 Bent
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: World Press Photo Exhibition 2026
picks.blurb: International photojournalism exhibition showcasing powerful images telling stories of our world from award-winning photographers.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Gohan
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Studio/K, LAB111, Rialto, Ketelhuis, Filmha
picks.blurb: Touching story of a stray dog whose life intersects with different people over ten years, exploring friendship and human-animal bonds.
picks.why: Emotionally moving film about unconditional love

picks.title: Monne
picks.blurb: Mediterranean restaurant with riverside terrace on the Amstel, boat dock and seasonal focus.

picks.title: Nara Nara
picks.blurb: New Egyptian restaurant by chef Said with Cairo flavors, Mediterranean touches and shared dining format.
picks.why: Fresh Egyptian cuisine on trendy square

picks.title: Bostheater
picks.blurb: Unique outdoor theater in Amsterdam forest offering performances, live music and family shows throughout summer.
picks.why: Outdoor theater in nature with family shows

picks.title: ENTR VR MUSEUm
picks.blurb: Travel back to 17th-century Amsterdam via VR headset with other visitors, making choices that shape your journey.

picks.title: The Jollof Club
picks.blurb: West-African restaurant by chef Nicolas Aquaa with modern takes on cuisine from Ghana, Nigeria, Senegal.

picks.title: Cinema Culinair
picks.blurb: Watch food-themed films while eating dishes from the screen, served with precision timing in brunch or seven-course dinner format.
picks.why: Film experience using all five senses

picks.title: Kay Slice QUANZA
picks.blurb: Free outdoor music performance in Vondelpark; bring a blanket and picnic to enjoy the summer atmosphere.

picks.title: TREK Foodfestival
picks.blurb: Open-air food festival with trucks, live entertainment, cocktails and dance music after sunset.
picks.why: Free food festival with live music

picks.title: De Hortus Zomeravonden
picks.blurb: Extended summer Sunday with blooming gardens, tropical greenhouses, live music and golden hour walks.
picks.why: Beautiful garden open late with live music

picks.title: Homelanding Zomerfestival
picks.blurb: Five-day free festival with live music, food, workshops, games and kids' activities across the waterfront.

picks.title: Ekō – Japan in twee beeldverhalen
picks.blurb: Japanese visual narratives exhibition at the Maritime Museum exploring cultural connections through maritime history.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Nederland – Japan. Door de tijd verbonden
picks.blurb: Explore Japan-Netherlands cultural exchange through centuries with rare objects, screens, swords and lacquerware.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Martine Gutierrez – Wunderkind
picks.blurb: Personal exploration of identity and self-expression through new photos, childhood objects and handmade works by artist Gutierrez.

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End musical based on true story of Henry Fraser; emotional, hopeful tale of life-changing moments performed on iconic Carré stage.
picks.why: Inspiring West End musical at Carré

picks.title: Amsterdam Gay Men's Chorus – Queer Celebration
picks.blurb: 100+ singers and dancers from Amsterdam's LGBTQ+ community deliver campy, defiant, radical celebration of queer resistance.

picks.title: Kwaku Festival
picks.blurb: Multicultural festival with En Vogue, Kassav', Broederliefde, live music, food, sport and culture.

picks.title: Julidans Festival
picks.blurb: International contemporary dance festival with innovative choreography and global perspectives on movement.
picks.why: Major arts festival spanning the weekend

picks.title: The Thing
picks.blurb:
A science fiction horror about paranoia and betrayal
 In John Carpenter's The Thing (1982), a group of American scientists at an isolated research station in th

picks.title: Agatha's Almanac
picks.blurb:
Life on the farm in pictures
 In the documentary Agatha's Almanac , director Amalie Atkins follows her 90-year-old aunt Agatha on the old family farm in Manitob

picks.title: Boom Chicago Comedy Festival
picks.blurb: High and low-brow international comedy with workshop opportunities. Top performers; expect verbal wit and laughter.

picks.title: Showgirls
picks.blurb:
From Hollywood flop to cult classic
 Paul Verhoeven's Showgirls (1995) is considered one of the most notorious Hollywood productions of the 1990s. The film flop

picks.title: De Roode Remise Singing Session
picks.blurb:
De Roode Remise Singing Session
 The core singers know each other from the famous Backroom Sessions at Mulligan's Irish Bar, but everyone, experienced or inexpe

picks.title: Lunchconcerten in de Westerkerk
picks.blurb:
Lunch concerts 
 For many years, the Westerkerk has hosted weekly lunch concerts in which the Duyschot organ excels under the hands of young top talents. Baroqu

picks.title: Nicolas van Poucke - Pianorecital
picks.blurb:
Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke
 Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke returns to the Kleine Zaal, where he continues his remarkable recital tradition. Praised by

picks.title: Lunchtime organ concerts at Waterlooplein
picks.blurb:
A moment of music in the middle of the day
 Step inside the Moses and Aaron Church and leave the hustle and bustle of the city behind for a moment. These luncht

picks.title: R&B, Hip Hop, Latin & Caribbean Vibes
picks.blurb:
Nightlife
 This evening offers a mix of musical styles. You will experience an atmosphere of old-school R B, hip-hop, and Latin American and Caribbean influence

picks.title: Festival TREK Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Netherlands' largest travelling food festival transforms the park into an open-air restaurant with global street food, live music, cabaret and disco.
picks.why: Free entry, food trucks from around world, live entertainmen
picks.source: I amsterdam / Festival Trek official · AmsterdamSpotted

picks.title: Cheeky Monday
picks.blurb:
Always a cool line-up!
 The Amsterdam crew runs one of the longest running club nights in Europe here, and is known worldwide for its great atmosphere, thanks i

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb:
Art, craft and design
 Museum Market takes place every month, every 3rd Sunday of the month (in October the 4th Sunday of the month). Between the trees near the

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati

picks.title: Art Amsterdam Spui
picks.blurb:
Discover art that truly touches you
 At Art Amsterdam Spui, artists present and sell their work themselves — straight from the studio and in direct contact with

picks.title: BOPS queer. pop. party
picks.blurb:
BOPS queer. pop. party
 Lineup: Resident DJs TMORGZ and Absoluut + various guest DJs
 BOPS is a space for the queer community and allies to dance the night away

picks.title: Throwback AMSTERDAM
picks.blurb: Nostalgia-driven dancefloor party celebrating 80s, 90s, and 00s hits. Second edition of Barcelona import.

picks.title: SPIELRAUM at CLUB RAUM
picks.blurb: Queer weekender returns with banging techno, electro, and acid across multiple DJs.

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Ten Katemarkt
picks.blurb: A proper everyday neighbourhood market off the tourist track — heaped produce, street food and Oud-West locals doing the shop.

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Lindengrachtmarkt
picks.blurb: The Jordaan’s beloved Saturday market — farm cheese, flowers and Amsterdam’s best market-stall fish, all down a leafy street.

picks.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
picks.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.
